name: Beamable Blog
description: Official Beamable blog covering game backend development, SDK releases, LiveOps, and platform updates.

posts.title: Mobile Monetization: the Old Playbook is Caving
posts.description: Mobile gaming is an $82 billion market that grew less than inflation last year. The broader mobile economy surged 10% while Gaming managed 1.3%. In real purchasing-power terms, that's not growth.

posts.title: Beamable at GDC 2026: Pulling back the Curtain
posts.description: Beamable is heading to San Francisco as part of the Skillz family following the February acquisition. The combined teams are developing a comprehensive solution for game developers.

posts.title: Unity SDK 5.0: Stability and Unity 6.3 LTS
posts.description: Version 5.0 emphasizes stability and support, including Dotnet 10 compatibility, Unity 6.3 engine support, and Beamable CLI 7.0, plus bug fixes and quality improvements.

posts.title: Beamable at D.I.C.E. 2026
posts.description: Following Skillz's acquisition of Beamable's technology, leadership will attend D.I.C.E. 2026 to discuss game development and present their backend toolkit for implementing meta-game features.

posts.title: Beamable's Technology Acquired by Skillz in Strategic Acquisition
posts.description: Beamable's technology has been acquired by Skillz, the leading skill-based competition platform in mobile games and the first mobile esports company listed on the NYSE.

posts.title: Best Backend for Cross-Platform Development: Why Studios Choose Beamable
posts.description: Beamable enables studios to connect players across mobile, PC, and console through one unified backend, managing identity, data, and live-ops in a single system.

posts.title: Hello, Beamable Venus
posts.description: Beamable Venus is the next flagship release, marking a significant step in delivering developer-focused game tooling with major improvements across microservices and capabilities.

posts.title: Best Backend for Multiplayer Games: Why Developers Build Faster with Beamable
posts.description: Beamable powers real-time, social, and competitive multiplayer experiences without requiring developers to manage servers or infrastructure, simplifying backend development.

posts.title: The Next Era of Beamable Is Coming: Introducing Beamable Venus
posts.description: Beamable Venus, arriving later in November 2025, includes upgrades for building, launching, and scaling live games faster across Unity, Unreal, and Web platforms.

posts.title: Best Backend for Web3 Games: Why Developers Choose Beamable
posts.description: Beamable enables developers to integrate blockchain features including digital ownership and authentication while maintaining the simplicity of a modern game backend.
